sarga.title: Sri Rama slays rakshasas and protects the sacrifice of Viswamitra

shlokas.original:
अथ तौ देशकालज्ञौ राजापुत्रावरिन्दमौ।
देशे काले च वाक्यज्ञावब्रूतां कौशिकं वच:।।1.30.1।।
shlokas.translation: Thus spoke the two princes to Viswamitra. They were conscious of place and time to speak and capable of conquering enemies.

shlokas.original:
भगवन् श्रोतुमिच्छावो यस्मिन् काले निशाचरौ।
संरक्षणीयौ तौ ब्रह्मन्नातिवर्तेत तत्क्षणम्।।1.30.2।।
shlokas.translation: "O adorable Brahmarshi, we want to know around what time the rakshasas will make their appearance so that the sacrifice can be protected. Let  not that moment pass".

shlokas.original:
एवं ब्रुवाणौ काकुत्स्थौ त्वरमाणौ युयुत्सया।
सर्वे ते मुनय: प्रीता: प्रशशंसुर्नृपात्मजौ।।1.30.3।।
shlokas.translation: The sages (of the asrama) were very pleased. They extolled the princes of Kakustha dynasty, who were eager to fight the rakshasas.

shlokas.original:
अद्यप्रभृति षड्रात्रं रक्षतं राघवौ युवाम्।
दीक्षां गतो ह्येष मुनिर्मौनित्वं च गमिष्यति।।1.30.4।।
shlokas.translation: "O sons of Raghu's lineage protect the sacrifice for six nights from today. With the ceremoney initiated, the sage would  observe silence said the inmates".

shlokas.original:
तौ तु तद्वचनं श्रुत्वा राजपुत्रौ यशस्विनौ।
अनिद्रौ षडहोरात्रं तपोवनमरक्षताम्।।1.30.5।।
shlokas.translation: At these words of the sages the illustrious princes got ready to protect the tapovana for six days and six nights without sleep.

shlokas.original:
उपासाञ्चक्रतुर्वीरौ यत्तौ परमधन्विनौ।
ररक्षतुर्मुनिवरं विश्वामित्रमरिन्दमौ।।1.30.6।।
shlokas.translation: The two mighty, heroic archers (Rama and Lakshmana), destroyers of enemies  attended upon the sacrifice in order to protect sage Viswamitra.

shlokas.original:
अथ काले गते तस्मिन् षष्ठेऽहनि समागते।
सौमित्रिमब्रवीद्रामो यत्तो भव समाहित:।।1.30.7।।
shlokas.translation: Time passed. When the sixth day arrived Rama cautioned Lakshmana to be vigilant.

shlokas.original:
रामस्यैवं ब्रुवाणस्य त्वरितस्य युयुत्सया।
प्रजज्वाल ततो वेदिस्सोपाध्यायपुरोहिता।।1.30.8।।
shlokas.translation: While he was fracing up for a fight, the fire on the sacrificial altar with the officiating priests and spiritual guides suddenly brightened.

shlokas.original:
सदर्भचमसस्रुक्का ससमित्कुसुमोच्चया।
विश्वामित्रेण सहिता वेदिर्जज्वाल सर्त्विजा।।1.30.9।।
shlokas.translation: The altar with Viswamitra and the officiating priests, kusa grass, ladles and square  vessels, bundles of sticks and  flowers, (used in the sacrificfial fire) got brightened up.

shlokas.original:
मन्त्रवच्च यथान्यायं यज्ञोऽसौ सम्प्रवर्तते।
आकाशे च महान् शब्द: प्रादुरासीद्भयानक:।।1.30.10।।
shlokas.translation: While the sacrifice was being performed in accordance with mantras, a great sound causing fear emerged from the sky.

shlokas.original:
आवार्य गगनं मेघो यथा प्रावृषि निर्गत:।
तथामायां विकुर्वाणौ राक्षसावभ्यधावताम्।।1.30.11।।
shlokas.translation: Like the clouds in the rainy season, the two rakshasas (Maricha and Subahu), covering the sky through their power of illusion, came running (to the sacrificial place).

shlokas.original:
मारीचश्च सुबाहुश्च तयोरनुचराश्च ये।
आगम्य भीमसङ्काशा रुधिरौघमवासृजन्।।1.30.12।।
shlokas.translation: Maricha and Subahu of fearful appearance arrived with their followers and rained streams of blood (on the sacrificial altar).

shlokas.original:
सा तेन रुधिरौघेण वेदिर्जज्वाल मण्डिता।
सहसाऽभिद्रुतो रामस्तानपश्य त्ततो दिवि।।1.30.13।।
shlokas.translation: Flames rose on the sacrificial altar drenched by the stream of blood. Immediately Rama rushed forward and beheld them in the sky.

shlokas.original:
तावापतन्तौ सहसा दृष्ट्वा राजीवलोचन:।
लक्ष्मणं त्वभिसम्प्रेक्ष्य रामो वचनमब्रवीत्।।1.30.14।।
shlokas.translation: Having seen them (Maricha and Subahu) suddenly advancing the lotuseyed Rama gazed at Lakshmana and said:

shlokas.original:
पश्य लक्ष्मण दुर्वृत्तान् राक्षसान् पिशिताशनान्।
मानवास्त्रसमाधूताननिलेन यथा घनान्।।1.30.15।।
shlokas.translation: "O Lakshmana, behold, by manavaastra I shall chase, like clouds scattered by winds those wicked rakshasas who eat human flesh".

shlokas.original:
मानवं परमोदारमस्त्रं परमभास्वरम्।
चि���्षेप परमक्रुद्धो मारीचोरसि राघव:।।1.30.16।।
shlokas.translation: The inflamed Rama hurled the highly powerful and effulgent Manavaastra on the chest of Maricha.

shlokas.original:
स तेन परमास्त्रेण मानवेन समाहित:।
संपूर्णं योजनशतं क्षिप्तस्सागरसम्प्लवे।।1.30.17।।
shlokas.translation: Smitten by that powerful Manavaastra, Maricha was thrown to a full distance of hundred yojanas into the surging high seas.

shlokas.original:
विचेतनं विघूर्णन्तं शीतेषु बलताडितम्।
निरस्तं दृश्य मारीचं रामो लक्ष्मणमब्रवीत्।।1.30.18।।
shlokas.translation: Having seen Maricha beaten by the power of that coolarrowed astra, thrown to a distance, reeling and lying unconscious, Rama spoke to Lakshmana:

shlokas.original:
पश्य लक्ष्मण शीतेषुं मानवं धर्मसंहितम्।
मोहयित्वा नयत्येनं न च प्राणैर्व्ययुज्यत।।1.30.19।।
shlokas.translation: "O Lakshmana behold this Manavaastra associated with righteousness employing a cool arrow, which carries him away by making him unconscious and yet not taking his life.

shlokas.original:
इमानपि वधिष्यामि निर्घृणान् दुष्टचारिण:।
राक्षसान् पापकर्मस्थान् यज्ञघ्नान् रुधिराशनान्।।1.30.20।।
shlokas.translation: "I shall slay these merciless, wicked, doers of sinful acts, destroyers of sacrifices and blood-thirsty rakshasas".

shlokas.original:
सङ्गृह्यास्त्रं ततो रामो दिव्यमाग्नेयमद्भुतम्।
सुबाहूरसि चिक्षेप सविद्ध: प्रापतद्भुवि।।1.30.21।।
shlokas.translation: Then Rama took hold of the celestial, marvellous Agneya weapon and hurled it into the chest of Subahu. Struck, Subahuhe fell down on the ground.

shlokas.original:
शेषान् वायव्यमादाय निजघान महायशा:।
राघव: परमोदारो मुनीनां मुदमावहन्।।1.30.22।।
shlokas.translation: Illustrious and highly generous Rama killed the  remaining rakshasas  with the Vayavya weapon to the delight of the ascetics.

shlokas.original:
स हत्वा राक्षसान् सर्वान् यज्ञघ्नान् रघुनन्दन:।
ऋषिभि: पूजितस्तत्र यथेन्द्रो विजये पुरा।।1.30.23।।
shlokas.translation: Having killed the rakshasas, obstructors of sacrifices, Rama was worshipped by the hermits like Indra was honoured in the past when he was victorious.

shlokas.original:
अथ यज्ञे समाप्ते तु विश्वामित्रो महामुनि:।
निरीतिका दिशो दृष्टवा काकुत्स्थमिदमब्रवीत्।।1.30.24।।
shlokas.translation: Thereafter, when the sacrifice was completed, maharshi Viswamitra beholding all the directions  bereft of evils, said to Rama:

shlokas.original:
कृतार्थोऽस्मि महाबाहो कृतं गुरुवचस्त्वया।
सिद्धाश्रममिदं सत्यं कृतं राम महायश:।।1.30.25।।
shlokas.translation: "O mightyarmed Rama I am fulfilled. Your father's commands have been executed. O  Illustrious one, Siddhaashrama has proved true to its name". इत्यार्षे श्रीमद्रामायणे वाल्मीकीय आदिकाव्ये बालकाण्डे त्रिंशस्सर्ग:।।02 Thus ends the thirtieth sarga of Balakanda of the holy Ramayana the first epic composed by sage Valmiki.
